raw_consumer_statement:
I am disputing a charge of {$650.00} from BUDGET RENT A CAR ( Case ID # XXXX ), which Citibank has XXXX to correct despite clear evidence of Merchant 's billing or clerical error. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, a XXXX agent ( XXXX ) created duplicate rental agreements ( # XXXX and # XXXX ) for the same vehicle ( Plate : XXXX, Vehicle No : XXXX, Vehicle : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ). XXXX 's own system closed the agreement with # XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX at XXXX PM, acknowledging the vehicle was returned and generated receipt for {$120.00} and charged my card ( Please refer to attachment " XXXX '' ). However, XXXX erroneously kept the second agreement ( # XXXX ) open until XX/XX/XXXX, charging me {$650.00} for a 'late return ' with receipt showing return time of XX/XX/XXXX XXXX pm. ( Please refer to attachment " XXXX '' ) Despite providing these details, Citibank denied my dispute, stating they found no evidence the charge was 'paid for by other means ' ( Please refer to " XXXX '' ). This is a failure to investigate the actual issue - " Duplicate Billing ''. Furthermore, I have photo of vehicle dashboard showing the mileage and fuel status ( please refer to " XXXX '' ), and a physical return slip where the agent, XXXX, hand-wrote Agreement # XXXX at checkout, and I personally documented the return date & time as XX/XX/XXXX XXXX PM on that same slip when I dropped the car off on XX/XX/XXXX ( Please refer to " XXXX '' ). Citibank is ignoring the physical impossibility of a merchant charging for a 'late return ' on a vehicle their own records show was returned days earlier.
